overview: Gruul Basking Broodscale Combo is a fast, disruptive combo deck that aims to resolve Glaring Fleshraker and Malevolent Rumble repeatedly or in tandem to force opponent sacrifices, leveraging Kozilek’s Command for flexible interaction and Basking Broodscale as the combo engine to multiply threats. It excels against midrange and combo decks that rely on board presence or specific win conditions, while struggling against hyper-aggressive decks that can race it before the combo locks in.
win_conditions: Glaring Fleshraker and Malevolent Rumble are the primary finishers.
Once you can cast them repeatedly or in response to threats, the game is often over.
Basking Broodscale enables this by copying triggers or creating additional threats.
Kozilek’s Command can finish low-health decks with its 5 damage option.

keep_criteria: Keep any hand with 2-3 lands plus at least one combo piece (Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, or Malevolent Rumble) and a way to protect or enable it (Kozilek’s Command, cheap acceleration, or removal).
Prioritize keeping hands that can play a land, cast a threat, and have a plan for turn 2-3.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan on 3+ lands with no threats, 2+ lands with only removal and no combo pieces, or hands with 4+ lands.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, ideally a dual or shock land if the format supports it, or a basic that taps for R or G.
If you have a cheap combo piece or acceleration, play it.
Turn 2: Play a second land, then cast your first Glaring Fleshraker or Malevolent Rumble.
If you have Basking Broodscale, aim to cast it on turn 2 or 3 to trigger or copy the sacrifice effects.
Use Kozilek’s Command defensively (bounce a blocker or destroy a problematic permanent) or offensively (deal 5 damage to close out games against low-HP decks).
mid_game: Mana should be prioritized for casting threats and holding up Kozilek’s Command for interaction.
keep a threat in hand as insurance.

Manage your graveyard carefully—avoid unnecessary sacrifice triggers if you’re trying to combo off, and use Kozilek’s Command to bounce your own threats if needed to protect them from targeted removal.
Commit threats when the opponent has no interaction or when you can follow up with a second Fleshraker/Rumble.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers, targeted removal, or a clear win condition on the next turn.
Use Kozilek’s Command to bounce a key blocker or remove a problematic permanent before committing fully.
late_game: Glaring Fleshraker and Malevolent Rumble are the primary finishers.
Once you can cast them repeatedly or in response to threats, the game is often over.
Basking Broodscale enables this by copying triggers or creating additional threats.
Kozilek’s Command can finish low-health decks with its 5 damage option.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to resolving a Fleshraker/Rumble, the opponent has no interaction, or you can follow up with a second threat.
If you’re at 10+ life and the opponent is at 5 or less, use Kozilek’s Command to finish them.

sequencing_tips: Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board without follow-up, misusing Kozilek’s Command’s damage option when you need bounce, and failing to track opponent interaction.
Remember that Glaring Fleshraker and Malevolent Rumble are identical in function, so prioritize casting them in response to threats to maximize value.
Basking Broodscale’s combo interaction is key—practice trigger ordering to avoid unintended sacrifices.

matchups.advice: Use Kozilek’s Command to bounce your own threats or remove counterspells. Cast threats in response to their spells, or use cheap acceleration to outpace their mana. If possible, sideboard in additional protection or alternative win conditions.
Prioritize early interaction to disrupt their mana base. Use Kozilek’s Command to bounce or destroy key ramp pieces. If they get ahead, commit to your combo quickly before they can stabilize.

Stabilize with Kozilek’s Command bounce or removal. Mulligan aggressively for early interaction. Once stabilized, transition to your combo win condition. Don’t overcommit to trading; focus on surviving to turn 3-4.
Pressure with early threats and use Kozilek’s Command to remove their key answers. If they have sweepers, wait for them to use it, then commit. Use card advantage to outlast them.
Win the resource war. Prioritize keeping lands and early threats. Use Kozilek’s Command to bounce their combo pieces or remove their threats. Track their hand size and play around their interaction.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), targeted removal (e.g., Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares), and combo win conditions.
Pay attention to when the opponent taps out for a spell—they’re likely setting up a counter or a big play.
Track graveyard hate if they’re playing against Storm or Blink decks.
